bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just starting (time_fraction = 0.0), I will open with the my highest-utility offer to anchor the negotiation. This means choosing the highest-utility value ('valueE') for every issue, setting my initial utility to 1.0. This leaves room to concede on the less critical issues (like issueA and issueB) later if needed.

We are extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent offered a bid that gives me the absolute minimum utility across all issues. To show a willingness to negotiate while still aiming for near-maximum utility, I will make a very small concession on one of my least important issues (issueA), dropping it to valueD, while maintaining my ideal points for all other issues (yielding an overall utility of 0.98).

The opponent has repeated their initial opening offer consisting of their maximal values but giving me the reservation utility. As it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will continue making minor concessions on issues with the lowest importance (issue B) to demonstrate a willingness to compromise, while firmly holding onto my most highly weighted issues (issues D and E). I will propose valueD for issueB, bringing my overall utility to 0.96.

The negotiation is still very early (t = 0.06), but the opponent is currently repeating a highly unfavorable baseline offer across all issues (valueA). To encourage them to start moving, I will incrementally concede on my lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB) while standing firm on my high-fidelity issues (issueC, issueD, issueE). This offer maintains a high utility of 0.94 for me.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of valueA across all issues, which yields my minimum utility. Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08), I will incrementally concede on my lowest weight issues (issueB now dropping to valueC) while continuing to remain steadfast on my highest priority issues (issueC, issueD, issueE). This signals a willingness to negotiate while protecting my core utility.

The current time is very early (t=0.10). The opponent has repeatedly offered their maximum claim on all dimensions, giving me the lowest possible utility (valueA across all issues). I will continue to make measured concessions on lower-and-mid weight issues to encourage reciprocal movement, conceding slightly on issueC while holding firm on my most important issues, issueD and issueE.

The negotiation is still in the early stages at t=0.12. The opponent is continuously repeating their starting offer (all valueA) without making any concessions. To indicate willingness to negotiate while signaling that the opponent also needs to concede, I am making another minor concession on a lower-weight issue (issueB), keeping my utility high (0.86). I will maintain my positions on the most critical issues (issueD and issueE).

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.14), but the opponent is holding completely firm on their starting bid of 'valueA' for all issues. To encourage them to start conceding, I will make another small concession on a lower-weight issue, lowering 'issueA' from 'valueC' to 'valueB', while continuing to hold firm on my highest-priority issues ('issueD' and 'issueE' at 'valueE'). This keeps my overall utility high (0.84) while demonstrating a willingness to adjust on less critical points.

The opponent is holding firm on their initial offer of 'valueA' for all issues, which provides me with only 0.20 utility. As we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will make another small concession on the lowest-weight issue (issueA) to give them exactly what they are asking for on that specific issue, while continuing to hold extremely firm on my higher-weight issues (issueD and issueE). This brings my proposed offer's total utility to 0.82.

The opponent is rigidly holding to their initial proposal which minimizes my utility. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will concede entirely on my two lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB) by adopting their preferred values. This targeted concession signals cooperation and removes these issues from contention, shifting the burden onto the opponent to reciprocate on my high-weight issues (issueD and issueE).

The opponent has rigidly held to their absolute best offer without reciprocating any of my concessions. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.20), I will continue to signal my willingness to cooperate by making a small concession on issueC, which is of moderate importance to me. I will maintain my complete concession on issues A and B, while holding perfectly firm on issues D and E, which provide the vast majority of my overall utility.

The opponent has held strictly to their opening offer indicating a firm stance. I have already conceded entirely on my lowest weight issues (issueA and issueB), bringing them down to the opponent's preferred value. To keep the process moving while protecting my highest weight issues, I will now make a slight concession on issueC while holding firm on issueD and issueE.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their opening offer, holding firm on all issues. Given that we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will fully concede on my three lowest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to their preferred valueA, but I will strictly hold my ground on my most critical issues (issueD and issueE) at my highest preference. This creates a fair compromise and tests if they are willing to accept a highly asymmetric concession.

The opponent is holding firm on their opening offer of 'valueA' for all issues despite my repeated concessions on the lower-preference issues A, B, and C. Since the time fraction is only 0.26, I still have time, but to keep the negotiation moving, I will make a small concession on my higher-weight issue D while holding firm on issue E, targeting an overall utility of 0.62.

The opponent is holding strictly to their opening position of 'valueA' for every issue, without showing any flexibility or making any concessions. Since the time fraction is still comfortably early (t=0.28), I will significantly slow down my concessions. I have already accommodated their position on my lowest priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC), so I will hold those matching values while cautiously offering a minor concession on issueE alongside my previous concession on issueD to explore if they will accept a slightly more balanced offer while maintaining my utility above 0.55.

The opponent has rigidly maintained their opening offer across all turns, completely ignoring my significant concessions on issues A, B, and C. Since we are still early in the negotiation period (t=0.3), I must stop conceding and signal firmness. The opponent is holding perfectly firm with the same offer on every turn, which is their maximum and my minimum (utility 0.2 for me). The opponent is rigidly holding on to "valueA" for all issues, making no concessions so far. Given that we are still in the mid-game (t=0.42), I will maintain a firm stance on my most critical issues (issueD and issueE) while slightly reshuffling the minor issues to signal flexibility without dropping my utility further until they begin to concede.

The current time fraction is 0.46, meaning we are close to the midpoint. Since I have previously explored lower utilities down to ~0.56 without success, I will offer a moderate utility bid (0.64 for me) that gives them their preferred `valueA` on both low weight issues, while exploring `valueC` on issueC and issueD combinations to test their acceptance threshold.

The opponent has finally begun making concessions, changing their bid on issueE from valueA to valueB. I will maintain high utility targets for my most critical issues (`issueD` and `issueE`) to keep my overall expected value above 0.65, aiming to establish a fair mid-game tradeoff.
